CHAPTER 166
BETTING CONTROL

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Betting Control Act. Short title
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires- Interpretation
sections.text:
"authorised race meeting" means a race meeting authorised in terms of section
eighteen;
"the Board" means the Betting Control and Licensing Board established under this
Act;
"bookmaker" means any person who by way of business bets with members of the
public and includes any person operating a football pool;
"bookmaker's licence" means a licence issued to a bookmaker under the
provisions of this Act;
"licensed betting premises" means any premises duly licensed in terms of this Act
as premises wherein bets may be made and settled;
"Officer Commanding the Zambia Police Force" means the person holding the
office of the Inspector-General of Police in Zambia or any other person for
the time being holding office as the head of the Zambia Police Force;
"race-course" means any place used for the purpose of holding a race meeting;
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
"race meeting" means any gathering of the public or of the members of any
association of persons to watch horse races or any other class of sporting
event which may be declared by the Minister, by statutory notice, to be a
class of sporting event at which the Inspector General of Police shall be
entitled to authorise the attendance of bookmakers in accordance with the
provisions of section eighteen.
(As amended by G.N. No. 304 of 1964, Act 13 of 1974
and Act 8 of 1988)

sections.heading: (1) There shall be established a board to be known as the Betting Control and Establishment of
sections.text:
Licensing Board which shall consist of- Board
(a) a chairman, to be a person nominated by the Minister;
(b) the Inspector General or his authorised representative, being a police
officer not below the rank of Superintendent;
(c) such other persons, to a maximum of five, as the Minister appoints by
Gazette notice.
(2) The chairman and members of the Board, other than public officers, may at any
time be required by the Minister to resign from the Board.
(3) The chairman and members of the Board, other than public officers, shall be
paid out of moneys appropriated for the purpose by Parliament such remuneration and
allowances as the Minister may from time to time determine.
(4) The Minister may appoint a person to be secretary to the Board.
(5) A quorum of the Board shall be four.
(As amended by No. 5 of 1963)

sections.heading: (1) The Board may and is hereby authorised to issue licences in accordance with Issue of licences
sections.text:
the provisions of this Act and any regulations made thereunder.
(2) Any licence issued under the provisions of this Act may be varied, suspended,
cancelled or made the subject of any condition by the Board in accordance with the
provisions of this Act and any regulations made thereunder.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(3) The procedure of the Board in considering applications for licences and for
exercising any other powers under this Act shall be determined by regulations made by
the Minister under the provisions of this Act.
(As amended by G.N. No. 304 of 1964)

sections.heading: (1) No person shall carry on the business of a bookmaker otherwise than in Prohibition against
sections.text:
accordance with the provisions of a licence issued to him under this Act authorising him to unlicensed
carry on the said business at the premises named therein or at any race meeting bookmaking
authorised in terms of section eighteen:
Provided that nothing in this section shall apply to an employee of any licensed
bookmaker acting in the course of such employment at the licensed premises of such
bookmaker or at an authorised race meeting.
(2) In the case of a partnership of bookmakers, each partner shall require to be
licensed.
(3) Any person who carries on business as a bookmaker otherwise than in terms of
a licence issued under this Act shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on a first
conviction to a fine not exceeding one hundred penalty units and on any second or
subsequent conviction to a fine not exceeding five thousand penalty units
(As amended by Act 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) Any person who desires to obtain a licence under this Act shall make Application for
sections.text:
application in writing to the Board in the prescribed form and manner. licences
(2) Such application shall be accompanied by such information concerning the
applicant and the premises, if any, upon which he intends to carry on his business as a
bookmaker as may be prescribed.

sections.heading: (1) On receipt of an application in terms of section six, the Board may make such Examination and
sections.text:
investigations or require the submission of such further information as it may deem determination of
necessary in order to enable it to examine such application. licence applications by
Board
(2) After making such investigations and considering such information as may have
been required in terms of subsection (1), the Board shall, after considring the application
at a meeting held in accordance with regulations made under this Act, in its absolute
discretion either grant a licence to the applicant or refuse such licence without reason
given:
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
Provided that no licence shall be issued under this Act unless and until the Board
has satisfied itself that the applicant is a fit and proper person to hold such a licence and
that the premises in respect of which the application is made are suitable for the purpose.
(3) In considering the suitability of premises in accordance with the proviso to
subsection (2), the Board shall take into account the desirability of keeping such premises
at a proper and sufficient distance from premises licensed for the sale and consumption of
liquor.

sections.heading: (1) In every case where a licence is issued under this Act, the Board shall Imposition of
sections.text:
impose as a condition thereto the requirement that the applicant shall furnish the Board conditions by Board
with security by means of a fidelity bond or such other security as the Board may approve
to a sum of not less than four thousand kwacha.
(2) In determining the amount of such security, the Board shall take into account the
known business of the applicant and the amount, if any, by which such business may
reasonably be expected to increase in the ensuing year.
(3) The Board may at any time vary the amount of any security given in terms of this
section if it is satisfied that, having regard to the known scale of business of the
bookmaker concerned, such variation is reasonable.
(4) In the event of any bookmaker making default in the fulfilment of any financial
liability arising from bookmaking transactions, the Board shall have the power to realise
any security or part of such security given by the bookmaker under this section and to
apply any sum of money so realised to the satisfaction of such liability.
(5) In granting or renewing a licence under this Act, the Board may attach such
other conditions thereto as it thinks fit.
(6) Without prejudice to the generality of subsection (5), such conditions may relate
to the manner in which the bookmaker may conduct his business and to the suitability,
condition and conduct of the premises to which the licence applies and the hours during
which such premises may be open for business.

sections.heading: (1) In any case in which the Board is credibly informed that a bookmaker has Procedure where
sections.text:
made default in the payment of any debt arising from any bookmaking transaction lawfully bookmaker fails to pay
made under the provisions of this Act (hereinafter in this section referred to as a betting betting debts arising
from betting
debt), the Board shall in writing request the bookmaker to furnish such information as the
transactions
Board may require within such period as the Board shall specify.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(2) If it appears to the Board, on the information furnished by the bookmaker under
the provisions of subsection (1) or from information otherwise acquired by it, that all or any
of the security furnished in accordance with the provisions of section eight is likely to be
required to pay the betting debts of the bookmaker, the Board shall, after considering any
representations made to it by the bookmaker, publish once in the Gazette and once in a
newspaper circulating in the area in which the bookmaker carries on business a notice-
(a) stating that the security furnished under the provisions of section eight will
be realised and applied, subject to the provisions of this section, to the
payment of the betting debts of the bookmaker;
(b) inviting before such date (hereinafter in this section referred to as the
specified date) as may be specified in the notice the submission of claims
for the payment of such debts.
(3) Subject to the provisions of subsection (4), the Board shall pay any debts in
respect of which it has received notice in terms of this section and which it is satisfied are
due to the claimant.
(4) In any case in which the amount furnished as security under the provisions of
section eight is inadequate to pay the betting debts referred to in subsection (3), the Board
shall make pro rata payments of such debts.
(5) Nothing in this section shall preclude any person from recovering, in terms of
section twenty, a betting debt or part thereof which is not satisfied un

sections.heading: Every licence issued under this Act shall state the precise location and extent Requirements of
sections.text:
of the premises, if any, to which it relates and shall be endorsed with every condition licences
imposed by the Board in accordance with this Act.

sections.heading: (1) The Board may at any time during the subsistence of a licence issued under Suspension,
sections.text:
this Act for good cause suspend or cancel such licence, or vary any conditions imposed in cancellation and
relation to such licence at its discretion. variation of licence
(2) The Board shall not suspend for more than seven days nor cancel any licence
under the powers conferred by subsection (1) without giving the bookmaker concerned
reasonable opportunity to show cause why his licence should not be so suspended or
cancelled.

sections.heading: (1) Every licence issued or renewed after the 1st January, 1963, under this Act Duration and renewal
sections.text:
shall terminate on the 30th June which next occurs following the issue or renewal of such of licences
licence.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(2) Any bookmaker whose licence expires or is due to expire in terms of subsection
(1) who desires to renew such licence shall make application to the Board at such time
and in such form and manner as may be prescribed.
(3) In considering any application for a renewal of a licence, the Board may exercise
all or any of the powers conferred upon it by sections seven and eight and, after
complying with any procedure laid down in respect of renewal of licences by regulations
made under this Act, may in its absolute discretion either renew such licence or refuse
such renewal without reason given.
(As amended by No. 5 of 1963)

sections.heading: (1) Any licensed bookmaker who wishes at any time to transfer the conduct of Transfer of licensed
sections.text:
his business to premises other than those authorised in terms of his licence may apply to premises
the Board for authority so to do.
(2) Upon receipt of an application in accordance with subsection (1), the Board may
exercise any of the powers conferred upon it by sections seven and eight and may
thereupon vary the said licence so as to authorise the conduct of the bookmaker's
business at the premises the subject of the application in substitution for the premises
previously licensed.

sections.heading: Every bookmaker licensed under this Act shall be required to pay at the time of Licence fees
sections.text:
the issue of his licence a fee of one thousand fee units in the case of a first issue of a
licence and five hundred fee units in the case of each renewal thereof.
(As amended by Act No. 1 of 1994, 4 of 1992, 5 of 1993
and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) No person shall, except within licensed betting premises or at an authorised Prohibition against
sections.text:
race meeting, tout or otherwise personally solicit the patronage of members of the public touting
with any bookmaker.
(2) Any person who contravenes the provisions of subsection (1) shall be guilty of
an offence and shall be liable upon conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty penalty units.
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: Any person who shall upon any licensed betting premises sell or supply or Prohibition against
sections.text:
consume or permit the sale or supply or consumption of any alcoholic liquor shall be guilty liquor on licensed
of an offence and shall be liable to a fine not exceeding two hundred and fifty penalty premises
units.
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989)
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: Any person who shall permit upon any licensed betting premises the playing of Prohibition against
sections.text:
any game of chance shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable to a fine not playing games of
exceeding one hundred and fifty penalty units. chance
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) The promoters of any race meeting shall, if they desire bookmakers to be Authorisation of
sections.text:
permitted to carry on business at such race meeting, apply to the Inspector General of bookmaking at race
Police for a permit for that purpose. meetings
(2) Such application shall be in writing in the prescribed form.
(3) Upon receipt of an application in terms of subsection (1), the Inspector General
of Police may, after making such inquiries as he thinks fit, in his discretion issue a permit
authorising bookmaking to take place at the race meeting held on the race-course and on
the day or days specified in the said permit.
(4) Any person who permits a bookmaker to carry on business at any race meeting
otherwise than in accordance with this section shall be guilty of an offence and shall be
liable to a fine not exceeding one thousand penalty units.
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) No licensed betting premises shall be open for the transaction of business Closure of licensed
sections.text:
and no person shall make any bet in any licensed betting premises at any time between premises on race days
half an hour before the advertised time of the start of the first race and the advertised time
of the start of the last race of any authorised race meeting which takes place within ten
miles of such licensed betting premises.
(2) Any person who contravenes the provisions of subsection (1) shall be guilty of
an offence and shall be liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred penalty units.
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: It is hereby declared that any debt arising from any betting transaction lawfully Enforcement of betting
sections.text:
made under the provisions of this Act may be enforceable before the courts of Zambia in debts
the same manner as any other civil debt.

sections.heading: (1) Any police officer above the rank of Sub Inspector may, at any time during Police powers of entry
sections.text:
which the premises are open for business, enter any licensed betting premises and may
during any race meeting enter the race-course for the purpose of general police
supervision and may eject any drunken, noisy or disorderly persons found on such
premises or race-course.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia
(2) Any person who resists, hinders or obstructs any police officer in the exercise of Obstruction of Police
his powers under this section shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable to a fine not
exceeding five hundred penalty units or to imprisonment not exceeding three months.
(As amended by Act No. 24 of 1977, 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) No bookmaker and no servant or agent of any bookmaker shall make or No betting with
sections.text:
permit to be made any bet with any person under the age of eighteen. persons under
eighteen
(2) No bookmaker and no servant or agent of any bookmaker shall allow any person
under the age of eighteen to come upon licensed betting premises, whether for the
purpose of betting or otherwise.
(3) Any person who contravenes the provisions of this section shall be guilty of an
offence and shall be liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred penalty units.
(As amended by Act No. 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: The Board may at any time and shall, not less frequently than once in every Submission of
sections.text:
twelve months, require a licensed bookmaker to submit to the Board a properly audited bookmakers' accounts
statement of the bookmaker's accounts.

sections.heading: No licence issued under this Act shall be transferable to any person, and any Licences not to be
sections.text:
person who shall transfer or purport to transfer a licence shall be guilty of an offence and transferred
shall be liable to a fine not exceeding two thousand penalty units.
(As amended by 30 of 1989 and 13 of 1994)

sections.heading: (1) No bookmaker shall bet with any person on the results of any election of No betting on elections
sections.text:
persons to the National Assembly or to any public authority in Zambia.
(2) Any person who contravenes the provisions of this section shall be guilty of an
offence and shall be liable to a fine not exceeding one thousand penalty units.
(As amended by S.I. No. 91 of 1965 and 30 of 1989)

sections.heading: Any person aggrieved by a decision of the Board made under this Act may Appeals
sections.text:
appeal to the Minister.
(As amended by G.N. No. 304 of 1964)
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: Nothing in the Penal Code relating to the opening, keeping or use of common Non-application of
sections.text:
betting houses shall be deemed to apply to any licensed bookmaker lawfully acting under provisions of Penal
the provisions of this Act. Code relating to
common betting
houses.
Cap. 87

sections.heading: The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations- Regulations
sections.text:
(a) prescribing the form in which any application or other documents made
under this Act shall be made;
(b) regulating the procedure to be followed in the making of any application for
the issue, renewal or variation of a licence under this Act;
(c) regulating the procedure to be followed by the Board in exercising any
powers conferred upon it by this Act;
(d) providing for the advertisement of any application for a licence under this
Act and of any proceedings of the Board to consider and determine any
such application;
(e) providing for the right of any person interested to object to an application for
the issue, renewal or variation of a licence under this Act, and for the form
and manner of any such objection;
(f) p rescribing the form and manner in which statements of a bookmaker's
accounts shall be furnished to the Board in accordance with section
twenty-three;
(g) generally for the carrying into effect of the purposes of this Act.
(As amended by G.N. No. 304 of 1964)

sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as the Betting Control Regulations. Title
In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires- Interpretation
sections.text: "licence" means a licence issued to a bookmaker under the Act.

sections.heading: Every application for a licence shall be submitted to the Board in duplicate in Form of application
sections.text:
Form 1 set out in the Schedule.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: Every such application shall be submitted to the Board at least forty days before Time for submission of
sections.text: the date upon which it is desired to commence a bookmaking business. application

sections.heading: On receipt of any such application, the Board shall satisfy itself that the Further particulars
sections.text:
particulars required are set out with sufficient clarity and may return the application for
amplification or amendment.

sections.heading: On receipt of any such application, the Board shall forthwith, at the expense of Advertisement of
sections.text: the applicant, advertise the same in the Gazette and one local newspaper. application

sections.heading: Any person who wishes to object to the issue of a licence shall make such Objections
sections.text:
objections in writing to reach the Board within twenty-one days of the publication of the
advertisement in accordance with regulation 6 and shall set out the grounds therefor.

sections.heading: On receipt of any objection made in accordance with regulation 7, the Board Applicant to be
sections.text: shall forthwith send a copy of such objection to the applicant. informed of objection

sections.heading: At least ten days before considering any application for a licence, the Board Notice of meeting of
sections.text:
shall give notice of the meeting of the Board at which any such application is to be Board
considered, in such manner as it deems fit.

sections.heading: At any meeting at which an application is to be considered, the applicant and Objectors may be
sections.text:
any person objecting in accordance with these Regulations may appear either personally present or represented
or by counsel at the hearing and lead evidence in support of his application or objection, at the hearing
as the case may be.

sections.heading: At any meeting at which an application is to be considered, the Board shall Power to take
sections.text:
have power to take evidence on oath and the chairman is hereby authorised to administer evidence on oath
oaths for the purpose of this regulation.

sections.heading: The chairman shall preside at all meetings at which he is present. In the Chairman to preside
sections.text: absence of the chairman, the members shall elect one of their number to preside.

sections.heading: At any meeting the Board may decide either that the proceedings or any part of Meetings to be held in
sections.text: them shall be held in public or in private at its discretion. public or in private

sections.heading: Every meeting of the Board shall be held in accordance with the procedure set Procedure
sections.text:
out in these Regulations and in default of any prescribed provision the procedure
to be followed shall be laid down by the chairman.
Copyright Ministry of Legal Affairs, Government of the Republic of Zambia
The Laws of Zambia

sections.heading: Every application for a renewal of a licence shall be submitted to the Board in Renewal of a licence
sections.text: duplicate in Form 2 set out in the Schedule.

sections.heading: The procedure to be followed by a licensed bookmaker for any renewal or Procedure on renewal
sections.text:
variation of his licence shall, mutatis mutandis, follow that set out for an original or variation of a
application as provided by regulations 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11. licence

sections.heading: Every licence issued under these Regulations shall be in Form 3 set out in the Form of licence
sections.text: Schedule.

sections.heading: The promoters of any race meeting who desire to obtain permission from the Application to
sections.text:
Inspector General of Police for bookmakers to be permitted to carry on business at such Inspector General of
race meeting shall apply for such permission to the Inspector General of Police
Police in Form 4 set out in the Schedule.

sections.heading: Any person who makes a false statement in any application for a licence made, False statement
sections.text:
or a renewal or variation thereof, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on
conviction to a fine not exceeding seven hundred and fifty penalty units or to imprisonment
for a period not exceeding three months, or to both.
(As amended by Act No. 13 of 1994)
